title: Text Component
description: Display and style text, nest text elements, and understand platform differences.

slides.title: The `<Text>` Component 📝
slides.content:
# Displaying Text

In React Native, all text **must** be wrapped in a `<Text>` component. You cannot put raw text strings directly inside a `<View>`.

```jsx
// ❌ Wrong
<View>Hello</View>

// ✅ Correct
<View>
  <Text>Hello</Text>
</View>
```

`<Text>` supports nesting and styling inheritance:

slides.title: Nested Text
slides.content: You can nest `<Text>` components to apply different styles to parts of a sentence:
slides.codeSnippet:
<Text style={{ fontSize: 20 }}>
  I am normal text, 
  <Text style={{ fontWeight: 'bold', color: 'red' }}>and I am bold and red!</Text>
</Text>

slides.title: Text Styling 🎨
slides.content:
## Common Text Styles

* `color`
* `fontSize`
* `fontWeight` (normal, bold, 100‑900)
* `fontStyle` (normal, italic)
* `textAlign` (auto, left, right, center, justify)
* `textDecorationLine` (none, underline, line-through)
* `lineHeight`

Note: Some CSS properties like `text-transform` are not available; you need to transform the string in JavaScript.

quizData.question: What happens if you put a raw text string directly inside a `<View>` without a `<Text>` wrapper?
quizData.explanation: React Native requires all text to be inside a `<Text>` component; otherwise you'll get an error: 'Text strings must be rendered within a <Text> component'.

slides.title: Platform Specifics 📱
slides.content:
## iOS vs Android

Some text properties behave differently on each platform:

* `fontWeight` on Android only supports `'normal'` and `'bold'` (numeric values may not work).
* `fontFamily` – you need to include custom fonts per platform.
* `allowFontScaling` – whether to respect the device's font size settings (default true).

You can use `Platform.select` to apply platform‑specific styles.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: For long paragraphs, set `lineHeight` to improve readability. A common rule of thumb is `lineHeight = fontSize * 1.4`.
